$comment: Known advisories in the PUBLISHED dependency graph of @graphorin packages, reviewed and accepted with mitigation. The consumer-smoke published-peer-audit job fails on any high/critical advisory NOT listed here, and ALSO fails when a listed advisory stops appearing (stale entries must be removed together with their docs). Workspace-side scanners (osv-scanner over pnpm-lock.yaml) cannot see these: the workspace lockfile applies pnpm overrides that npm consumers never inherit.

advisories.reason: onnxruntime-node (every release up to 1.27.0) pins adm-zip ^0.5.16, below the patched 0.6.0; it reaches consumers via @graphorin/embedder-transformersjs -> @huggingface/transformers (which pins onnxruntime-node exactly). adm-zip is used ONLY in onnxruntime-node's install script to unzip its own release archives - the runtime never parses foreign ZIPs, so the practical exposure is an install-time DoS via a compromised download channel. The workspace itself is on adm-zip 0.6.0 via a pnpm override (PR #206); the documented consumer mitigation is an equivalent override in the consumer's root manifest - see documentation/guide/security.md.
advisories.removeWhen: onnxruntime-node widens its adm-zip range to >=0.6.0 AND @huggingface/transformers picks that release up.
